Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation

Student Laboratory

was established in order to ensure that students studying in the Department of Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation within the Faculty of Health Sciences of Üsküdar University reinforce their theoretical knowledge with applications. In every course that requires practice and skill, the relevant applications are explained to the students in accordance with the content of the vocational courses in the presence of expert lecturers.

Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation Student Laboratory Applications

Special evaluation techniques for patient and disease-specific treatment, special exercise and rehabilitation programs, heat-light-hydrotherapy, electrotherapy and manual application practices are taught in this laboratory. In addition to basic exercise and manual applications, this laboratory allows students to learn the rehabilitation contents for individuals with musculoskeletal system, nervous system, cardiopulmonary system diseases in the light of up-to-date information, accompanied by specialized teaching staff.

Practical courses in Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation Student Laboratory in addition to theoretical knowledge

Basic Measurement and Evaluation:

Many anthropometric examinations such as joint motion assessment, muscular strength assessment, flexibility assessment and flexibility, diameter, circumference, fat tissue thickness, which are used to determine the treatment program effectively in physiotherapy, are processed within basic measurement and evaluation.

Manipulative Therapy I:

Mechanical, physiological, psychological and reflex effects of unique therapeutic massage techniques, evaluation methods to be used to determine the effectiveness after application, practical applications, indications and contraindications of these methods are covered.

Electrotherapy I-II:

Determination of electrotherapeutic agents to support the physiotherapy and rehabilitation program, application methods and problem solutions are covered.

Heat, Light and Hydrotherapy:

Review of superficial temperature modalities and hydrotherapy methods used in physiotherapy and rehabilitation and basic information about physical principles, physiological effects, application methods, indications and contraindications, dangers and application examples are covered.

Basic Exercise Applications:

In this course, range of motion exercises, stretching exercises, strengthening exercises, isokinetic exercises, aerobic exercises, relaxation exercises, open and closed ring exercises, balance and deep sensory exercises, plyometric exercises, dynamic lumbar stabilization exercises, functional exercises and return to sport exercises are covered.

Manipulative Treatment II:

Functional anatomy, biomechanics and pathomechanics of joints and soft tissues, basic features of manipulation, mobilization and transverse friction techniques, basic measurement, evaluation and manual therapy applications in joint and soft tissue pathologies, clinical decision making and case examples are covered.

Neurophysiologic Approaches I:

The definition, basic principles and applications of proprioceptive neuromuscular facilitation (PNF) techniques in treatment and rehabilitation are covered.

Orthopedic Rehabilitation:

Basic orthopedic rehabilitation principles and current approaches in orthopedic physiotherapy and rehabilitation of musculoskeletal system problems followed conservatively and surgically.

Occupational Therapy:

The role of occupational therapy (occupational therapy) and occupational therapist (occupational therapist), basic assessment and appropriate approaches are examined.

Pediatric Rehabilitation:

Physiotherapy and rehabilitation-specific assessment and treatment methods applied in pediatric cases are covered.

Pulmonary Rehabilitation:

The basic principles of pulmonary physiotherapy and rehabilitation techniques, pathologies leading to respiratory system disorders and physiotherapy and rehabilitation approaches for them are discussed.

Neurophysiologic Approaches II:

Clinical features seen after hemiplegia (stroke) and neurophysiological approaches used in its treatment are covered.

Neurological Rehabilitation:

Clinical features in central and peripheral nervous system diseases, measurement and evaluation methods used in neurological rehabilitation and physiotherapy rehabilitation approaches are covered.

Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation In addition to the practical courses in the student laboratory, students can perform the majority of their clinical studies in their senior year at the NP Physiotherapy and Rehabilitation Center, which is located within the NP Feneryolu Medical Center and includes nine specialized units, where they can receive patients and see case examples under the supervision and supervision of academicians in the department.

This laboratory is used by the departments listed below.
